message:
Ever feel like your dog understands way more than they let on? You're not imagining it. 🐶

The average dog can learn around 250 words, signals, and gestures — putting them on par with a 2-year-old child. And some superstar pups have been documented recognizing over 1,000 different words. As a trainer, here's what that tells me: your dog is ALWAYS listening and learning, whether you're "officially" in a training session or not.

That's exactly why consistency matters so much. Every time you repeat a cue the same way and reward the right response, you add another word to their vocabulary. And every time you let a behavior slide, you're teaching them something too. 😉

So start small: pick ONE new word this week and use it the exact same way, every single time. You'll be amazed how fast it clicks.

How many words do you think YOUR dog actually knows? Comment 🐾 with the one cue they've completely mastered!
